qa_pairs.question: What transfer and synchronization options does Globus offer, and what should users know about encryption?
qa_pairs.answer: Globus provides several "Transfer & Sync Options," including syncing only new or changed files, deleting files on destinations that do not exist in the source, preserving source file modification times, verifying file integrity (which is on by default), and encrypting transfers. It is important to note that enabling encryption significantly reduces transfer performance, so it should only be used for sensitive data.

qa_pairs.question: What is Globus Connect Personal, and how do I install it on my computer?
qa_pairs.answer: Globus Connect Personal is a desktop client for Windows, macOS X, or Linux that makes it easy to transfer files to and from a personal computer. To install it, log into the Alliance Globus portal, click on the "Collections" icon from the File Manager screen, then click "Get Globus Connect Personal" in the top right. Download the link for your operating system and install Globus Connect Personal. The full endpoint name will be [your username]#[name you give setup].

qa_pairs.question: Which directories on Alliance general-purpose clusters support Globus sharing, and what is required to enable sharing on `/project`?
qa_pairs.answer: On Alliance general-purpose clusters, Globus sharing is enabled for the `/home` directory (except on Rorqual) and is not enabled for `/scratch` (except on Narval). For `/project`, sharing is on-demand. The Project Investigator (PI) needs to contact globus@tech.alliancecan.ca with confirmation, the cluster name, the path to enable, and whether the sharing will be read-only or allow both reading and writing.
